description: Reviewed exceptions to the tenant fail-open static audit (scripts/audit-tenant-queries.mjs). One entry per file. APPROVED = intentional platform/super-admin scope or shared reference data. PENDING-MIGRATION = known tenant-owned debt and a production blocker (NO-GO). Migrated modules are removed from this list so CI blocks any regression that reintroduces a fail-open pattern in them.

exceptions.reason: Platform/ops or cross-tenant reporting surface; guard throws for non-SUPER_ADMIN, so the ternary only relaxes scope for an authenticated SUPER_ADMIN with an explicit active tenant. Fail-open literal retained pending refactor to explicit platform-scope decorator.

Work-order taxonomy is dual-scoped reference data: entries are either tenant-owned or global (tenantId null) seed defaults shared across tenants, keyed by a __global__ cache bucket. tenantId ?? null selects the correct scope rather than dropping tenant filtering. All controller entry points pass the authenticated actor and the TenantContextGuard forces a resolvable tenant for non-SUPER_ADMIN, so the null (global) path is unreachable from tenant-user HTTP requests. Retained by design, not a tenant-owned data leak.

Tenant-owned business module not yet migrated to requireTenantId()/tenantWhere(). Guard enforces tenant presence at the HTTP boundary; explicit fail-closed migration still required. Tracked as production blocker (NO-GO).
